as a same-transaction price oracle.** Every getter on this interface      derives its result from `IPartyPool` view state (`balances`, `LMSR`, the BFStore      pointed at by `bfStore()`, `_protocolFeesOwed`) without read-only-reentrancy protection.      An integrator that reads      these values from inside a token callback (ERC777, ERC677, custom hook) or any other      mid-transaction callback path will observe inconsistent values. Treat the prices and      quotes returned here as point-in-time, off-chain\u2013consumable data. For on-chain pricing,      derive a TWAP from `Swap` / `Mint` / `Burn` event logs or use an external manipulation-      resistant oracle. See `IPartyPool` for the full guidance and `doc/security/checklist.md`      \u00a7C.2.","kind":"dev","methods":{"burnAmounts(address,uint256)":{"params":{"lpTokenAmount":"The amount of LP tokens to burn"},"returns":{"withdrawAmounts":"Array of token amounts that will be received"}},"burnSwapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"amount of LP tokens to burn","outputTokenIndex":"index of target asset to receive"}},"denominators(address)":{"details":"Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`. Anchored to the pool's `effectiveSigmaQ` (the block-aligned `min(\u03c3_swap, \u03c3_live)`      that swap()/swapMint()/burn() price against), so this is the *executable* marginal      price, not a live-\u03a3q theoretical price. This is what makes the exact-price ceiling      workflow in `swapAmountsForExactPrice` safe: both share the same execution anchor.      Represents the cost in input token units to acquire one unit of the output token.      Fee-free and infinitesimal \u2014 actual cost for a finite swap will be higher. Caller workflow:        uint256 target = info.price(pool, i, j) * 1005 / 1000; // 0.5% slippage ceiling        (uint256 maxIn, uint256 minOut,) = info.swapAmountsForExactPrice(pool, i, j, target);        pool.swap(payer, sel, recv, i, j, maxIn, minOut, deadline, false, \"\");","params":{"inputTokenIndex":"index of token being sold","maxPrice":"Q128.128 denomination-adjusted ceiling on P_fwd. Mirrors the on-chain swap math."},"swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Closed-form exact-output swap quote (companion to `swapAmounts` exact-in)."},"swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Off-chain view helper: bisect to find the exact input amount that drives the forward         marginal buy price P_fwd(input\u2192output) up to `maxPrice` (denomination-adjusted)."},"swapMintAmounts(address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Quote an exact-LP-out swap-mint: given a target lpAmountOut, return the         gross input (kernel + swap fee) the pool will pull from the caller. c/IPartyInfo.sol":{"keccak256":"0xc466708976e86bb802a77cca9c83533cbd55e793f712d964a7750bb159a6b340","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://40c3bb22125fe273b945345b81bae892e08e24cb5af5550e732416084e672989","dweb:/ipfs/QmUyY9YbNDNRN2Ct6tHJoGoco8CywWAeq7J7S6fG7sSxSv"]},"src/IPartyPool.sol":{"keccak256":"0xc83b499724c22f1d8555c305efba02b16e027cffa5c99c0487d2a2252e054843","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://7f53d0c7d6dab1ac590caec7fcbe47938d5c1c38c9d0c7624675f36c841652d6","dweb:/ipfs/QmcNdu8joAF4iLceHSA6prVFdoBryJLbgiVfC9Nowj9k32"]},"src/IPermit2.sol":{"keccak256":"0x06fd721baa37c2174a56cff301d8ca6f671cae49bd942e770d3ec880e8dd4cce","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://1c545d16c075e8d1ca8c27e042c270de34f505661525c079de53bac1cfa39af7","dweb:/ipfs/QmXjQ2schyXW77uziMiH77GENiFrmswxaPUNcDjSSXxc7J"]},"src/LMSRKernel.sol":{"keccak256":"0xbd2f87534e6c4ae46b986e9aba0b86e06662421d32d2b171d6ea4316faf70370","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://031afee975bc8974728768d8969d4b3ab5577cb419e43f20b039106ade0e15aa","dweb:/ipfs/QmQMa9gKNjq3iAs71tUkfrm4yihtTUUsuyUwvZGF5ArXRX"]},"src/NativeWrapper.sol":{"keccak256":"0x3c24a1271530ce97216e376105903115a938a44a2f3968747a708835d532d328","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://2abb6bd8905a21e13ccf344332210d3e9f6e18e6768acbe93eba30dca690d135","dweb:/ipfs/QmUibKxx3sgw3WxDNggkoo9chTP8E7HmHrkLhUBjRcc49D"]}},"version":1}

params.lpTokenAmount: The amount of LP tokens to burn

returns.withdrawAmounts: Array of token amounts that will be received

params.lpAmount: amount of LP tokens to burn
params.outputTokenIndex: index of target asset to receive

denominators(address).details: Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`.         Equivalent to `pool.denominators()` in the previous interface; moved here so that         PartyPool's deployed bytecode stays within EIP-170.

fees(address).details: Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`.

fetchPoolState(address).details: Same read-only-reentrancy caveat as the other quote helpers — do not use as      a same-transaction price oracle (see the interface-level NatSpec).

returns.amountInUsed: total uint input that will be transferred at that lpAmountOut
returns.lpAmountOut: largest feasible LP amount; 0 if budget is too small to mint anything

mintAmounts(address,uint256).details: Returns minimum token amounts (rounded up) to receive `lpTokenAmount` LP tokens      at current pool proportions. Returns zeros for the initial deposit (handled by      transferring tokens first, then calling `initialMint()`).

params.lpTokenAmount: The desired amount of LP tokens

returns.depositAmounts: Array of token amounts to deposit (rounded up)

poolPrice(address,uint256).details: Balanced approximation: pool value ≈ nAssets × quoteBalance.      Per-LP value = nAssets × quoteBalance / totalSupply / D[quote].

params.quoteTokenIndex: index of the quote asset in which to denominate the LP price

returns._0: price Q64.64 value equal to quote per LP token unit

price(address,uint256,uint256).details: Computed as `exp((q[input] − q[output]) / b) × D[input] / D[output]` where      `b = κ · effectiveSigmaQ` and `D[k] = denominators(pool)[k]`.      Anchored to the pool's `effectiveSigmaQ` (the block-aligned `min(σ_swap, σ_live)`      that swap()/swapMint()/burn() price against), so this is the *executable* marginal      price, not a live-Σq theoretical price. This is what makes the exact-price ceiling      workflow in `swapAmountsForExactPrice` safe: both share the same execution anchor.      Represents the cost in input token units to acquire one unit of the output token.      Fee-free and infinitesimal — actual cost for a finite swap will be higher.      On a balanced pool with equal denominators this returns exactly `1 << 128`.

params.inputTokenIndex: index of the token being sold
params.outputTokenIndex: index of the token being bought

returns._0: External buy price as Q128.128 uint256

params.maxAmountIn: exact input to transfer (fee is deducted from output, not added to input)

returns.amountOut: net output amount user would receive (gross output minus outFee)

swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint25.details: Given desired NET output `amountOut` (what the caller receives after fee), returns the      required input without iteration. Reverts if `amountOut` is infeasible.

params.amountOut: desired NET output in uint token units (after fee deduction)

returns.amountIn: total uint input required (no fee on input; fee is on output side)

swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256.details: Uses 64 iterations of bisection on the actual two-sided LMSR state. Returns      `(amountIn, minAmountOut, fee)` — pass both directly to `pool.swap()`.      NOT intended to be called from on-chain operations.      Caller workflow:        uint256 target = info.price(pool, i, j) * 1005 / 1000; // 0.5% slippage ceiling        (uint256 maxIn, uint256 minOut,) = info.swapAmountsForExactPrice(pool, i, j, target);        pool.swap(payer, sel, recv, i, j, maxIn, minOut, deadline, false, "");

params.maxPrice: Q128.128 denomination-adjusted ceiling on P_fwd.                         Must be strictly greater than info.price(pool, i, j).

returns.amountIn: exact input (uint token units; fee is on output side)
returns.amountOut: net output (uint token units); pass as `minAmountOut` to `swap()`

params.lpAmountOut: target LP shares the caller wants minted

returns.amountInUsed: total uint input that will be transferred (kernel input + inFee)
returns.inFee: swap-leg fee component included in amountInUsed (split LP / protocol)

burnAmounts(address,uint256).notice: Calculate the proportional withdrawal amounts for a given LP token amount.

burnSwapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Calculate the amounts for a burn swap operation.

denominators(address).notice: Per-token uint base denominators used to convert uint token amounts ↔ internal Q64.64.

fees(address).notice: Per-asset swap fees in ppm. For asset-to-asset swaps, the effective pair fee is the         sum of the two asset fees (each < 10,000 by constructor invariant).

fetchPoolState(address).notice: Bundle every field an off-chain quoter needs into one snapshot, so the         webapp / SDK can reconstruct the pool's pricing state from a single         RPC view call.

maxLpForBudget(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Off-chain helper for budget-style callers: bisect to find the largest         lpAmountOut that can be minted within `maxAmountIn`. View-only — not for         on-chain consumption. Does not account for the σ_swap gate or rate-limit         budget; callers should additionally call `quoteMint`/`quoteSwapMint` to         confirm the mint would land.

mintAmounts(address,uint256).notice: Calculate the proportional deposit amounts required for a given LP token amount.

poolPrice(address,uint256).notice: Price of one LP token denominated in `quoteToken` as Q64.64.

price(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Infinitesimal marginal buy price for a swap input→output as Q128.128,         denomination-adjusted to external token units.

swapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256,uint.notice: Quote an exact-input swap. Mirrors the on-chain swap math.

swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint25.notice: Closed-form exact-output swap quote (companion to `swapAmounts` exact-in).

swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256.notice: Off-chain view helper: bisect to find the exact input amount that drives the forward         marginal buy price P_fwd(input→output) up to `maxPrice` (denomination-adjusted).

swapMintAmounts(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Quote an exact-LP-out swap-mint: given a target lpAmountOut, return the         gross input (kernel + swap fee) the pool will pull from the caller.         There is no protocol mint fee in this design.

working(address).notice: Returns true iff the pool is not killed and has been initialized with liquidity.
